This biographical article relating to Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Muhammad_Irfan_Saeed_Bhatti&action=edit).

(born 18 November 1992) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He competed at the [2014](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Commonwealth Games") and [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2018 Commonwealth Games").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was the men's doubles champion at the 2016 All Pakistan National ranking tournament.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He also the runner-up at [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament in the singles and doubles events.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Teamed-up with [Azeem Sarwar](/wiki/Azeem_Sarwar_(badminton) "Azeem Sarwar (badminton)"), they won the men's doubles title at the Nepal Annapurna International Badminton Championship.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He was the bronze medalists at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the men's singles and team events.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

["Players: Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti"](http://bwfbadminton.com/player/95500/muhammad-irfan-saeed-bhatti). [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation"). Retrieved 12 November 2016.
["Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti Biography"](http://g2014results.thecgf.com/athlete/badminton/1000886/muhammad_irfan_saeed_bhatti.html). Glasgow 2014. Retrieved 12 November 2016.

["Participants: Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti"](https://results.gc2018.com/en/badminton/athlete-profile-n6025515-muhammad-irfan-saeed-bhatti.htm). Gold Coast 2018. Retrieved 12 April 2018.[*[dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

["Final Results National Ranking Tournament, Lahore 2016"](https://web.archive.org/web/20170729174813/http://www.pakbadminton.com/news/105531/FINAL-RESULTS-National-Ranking-Tournament-Lahore-2016). [Pakistan Badminton Federation](/wiki/Pakistan_Badminton_Federation "Pakistan Badminton Federation"). Archived from [the original](http://www.pakbadminton.com/news/105531/FINAL-RESULTS-National-Ranking-Tournament-Lahore-2016) on 29 July 2017. Retrieved 29 July 2017.

["Badminton: Pakistan fall short of clean sweep"](https://tribune.com.pk/story/1205801/badminton-pakistan-fall-short-clean-sweep/). [The Express Tribune](/wiki/The_Express_Tribune "The Express Tribune"). Retrieved 29 July 2017.

["Pakistan's Saeed, Sarwar beat Indian duo to clinch Nepal Badminton Championship"](http://nation.com.pk/sports/06-Jul-2017/pakistan-s-saeed-azeem-beat-indian-duo-to-clinch-nepal-badminton-championship). [The Nation](/wiki/The_Nation_(Pakistan) "The Nation (Pakistan)"). Retrieved 29 July 2017.
["XII South Asian Games"](http://www.sports.gov.pk/Participation/12th_SA_Games.html). [Pakistan Sports Board](/wiki/Pakistan_Sports_Board "Pakistan Sports Board"). Retrieved 16 December 2019.

["South Asian Games : Ruthvika Shivani Upset PV Sindhu To Win Gold"](http://xtratime.in/south-asian-games-ruthvika-shivani-upset-pv-sindhu-to-win-gold/). *xtratime.in*. 10 February 2016. Retrieved 16 December 2019.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player (born 1992)

Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti

**Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti** (born 18 November 1992) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He competed at the [2014](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2014 Commonwealth Games") and [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "Badminton at the 2018 Commonwealth Games").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was the men's doubles champion at the 2016 All Pakistan National ranking tournament.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He also the runner-up at [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament in the singles and doubles events.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Teamed-up with [Azeem Sarwar](/wiki/Azeem_Sarwar_(badminton) "Azeem Sarwar (badminton)"), they won the men's doubles title at the Nepal Annapurna International Badminton Championship.[[6]](#cite_note-6) He was the bronze medalists at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the men's singles and team events.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
